title: Masked Language Modeling (BERT style)
concept: Callback to Unit 275: BERT masks random tokens to extract bidirectional context; modern generative LLMs favor causal autoregressive loss for generation.

exercises.prompt: What is the main concept behind BERT's Masked Language Modeling?

options.text: Masking random words to learn bidirectional context

exercises.explain: Masked language modeling (MLM) hides words within a sentence to teach the AI context.

cards.title: The Hidden Word Game
cards.body: Have you ever played a game where you have to guess a hidden word in a sentence? You look at the words before and after the blank to find clues. This simple trick is how one of the most famous AI models, BERT, learned to understand human language so well!

cards.title: What is Masked Language Modeling?
cards.body: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) is a training method where we hide (mask) random words in a sentence and ask the AI to guess them. Because it can see the words on both the left and right of the hidden word, the AI learns deep, bidirectional context about how language works.

cards.title: The Fill-in-the-Blank Test
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a sentence with a word crossed out in black marker, like 'The cat sat on the [???]'. To guess the missing word, you look at both the words before and after the blank. This is exactly how BERT works! Traditional generative models only look at words to the left, like trying to guess the next word at the end of a sentence without seeing the rest.

cards.title: BERT in Action
cards.body: Sentence: 'The scientist looked through the [MASK] to see the stars.' \n\nBy looking at 'scientist' and 'stars', the AI learns that the hidden word [MASK] is likely 'telescope'. It uses clues from both directions!

cards.title: Is BERT a Creative Writer?
cards.body: Many believe that because BERT is great at understanding language, it is also great at writing essays. Actually, BERT's masked approach makes it poor at generating new text. Modern chatbots use 'causal' models that predict the very next word to write fluently.

cards.title: Two Ways to Read
cards.body: See the difference in how these two AI training styles read a sentence.

left.text: Looks at both left and right sides to guess a middle word. Great for understanding.

right.text: Looks only at the left side to predict the next word. Great for generating text.

exercises.prompt: How does BERT hide words during training?

options.text: It replaces random words with a [MASK] token.
It deletes the second half of every sentence.
It translates the sentence into another language first.
It adds extra words at the end of the sentence.

exercises.explain: BERT replaces random words with a special [MASK] token so the AI can practice guessing them using surrounding clues.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Masked language models can look at words that come after the blank to help guess the hidden word.
exercises.explain: Masked language models look at both past and future words in a sentence to understand context, whereas causal generative models only look at past words.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how BERT looks at context in both directions.
exercises.sentence: BERT uses a ________ approach to look at words on both sides of the mask.
exercises.explain: Because BERT can look at the words before and after a masked token, it is called a bidirectional model.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is a modern generative AI rather than a masked language model?

options.text: System A: Replaces 15% of the words in a book with blank spaces and tries to fill them in.
System B: Reads a prompt and writes the next word, one by one, to finish a story.
System C: Looks at a complete sentence with a hidden middle word and guesses the hidden word.

exercises.explain: System B is a classic causal LLM that only generates text by looking at previous words. Systems A and C represent bidirectional masked language modeling (like BERT).

exercises.prompt: Why do modern generative LLMs like GPT use causal autoregressive loss instead of BERT's masked language modeling for writing stories?

options.text: BERT generates text faster because it looks both ways at the same time.
Generative LLMs use masking to hide entire paragraphs from the user.
BERT masks random words to learn context, while generative LLMs predict the next word.
Masked modeling is only used to translate languages.

exercises.explain: BERT's bidirectional masking is fantastic for understanding context, but it cannot easily generate long, fluent text because it predicts masked words rather than generating one new word after another. Modern generative models use causal autoregressive training to generate text.
